**Robert Irwin Chartoff** (August 26, 1933 – June 10, 2015) was an American film producer and [philanthropist](/wiki/Philanthropist "Philanthropist").

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_Chartoff&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Chartoff was born on August 26, 1933, in New York City, the son of Bessie and William Chartoff.[[1]](#cite_note-filmr-1) His family was Jewish.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) He graduated from [Union College](/wiki/Union_College "Union College") in 1955, followed by [Columbia University](/wiki/Columbia_University "Columbia University") Law School.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_Chartoff&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Chartoff produced more than 30 movies, including the *[Rocky](/wiki/Rocky_(franchise) "Rocky (franchise)")* film series. He and fellow producer [Irwin Winkler](/wiki/Irwin_Winkler "Irwin Winkler") won an [Academy Award for Best Picture](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Picture "Academy Award for Best Picture") for its 1976 debut installment, *[Rocky](/wiki/Rocky "Rocky")*.

## Philanthropy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_Chartoff&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Philanthropy")]

Chartoff established the RC Charitable Foundation in 1990 to award grants to international schools and other child agencies. He served as its President. The RC Charitable Foundation gives grants awards to the Buddha Educational Trust. He served on the Community Advisory Board of the Younes and Soraya Israel Studies Center at UCLA.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_Chartoff&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Chartoff's first wife was Phyllis Raphael, with whom he had three children – Jenifer, William and Julie – before divorcing. In 1970, he married British actress [Vanessa Howard](/wiki/Vanessa_Howard "Vanessa Howard"), with whom he had one son, Charley. That marriage also ended in divorce. He last married Jenny Weyman, with whom he had one daughter, Miranda. He died at his home in [Santa Monica, California](/wiki/Santa_Monica,_California "Santa Monica, California"), in 2015 from [pancreatic cancer](/wiki/Pancreatic_cancer "Pancreatic cancer"), leaving a widow.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_Chartoff&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Filmography")]
